topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion. My heart gave a sudden, heavy thump against my ribs. I clicked the link, and the words swam into focus: my piece, my carefully crafted narrative, attributed to a complete stranger. A slow, hot flush began to creep up my neck, staining my cheeks. I leaned back in my chair, the leather groaning softly beneath me, my fingers tracing the edge of the desk. A low hum vibrated in my chest, a strange, electric current spreading outward. I stared at the screen, the feeling tightening in my core, demanding attention. I had to know who had done this.

[story 2]

"No. That can't be right." My voice was a tight whisper, laced with disbelief as I reread the publication details. The reality hit me like a physical blow. Someone else had stolen my voice, my words, and now they were being celebrated under a different banner. A warmth bloomed low in my stomach, a dizzying sensation that made my breath catch. I ran a hand through my hair, feeling the damp strands stick to my forehead. Every nerve ending seemed hyper-sensitive, prickling with a restless energy. I felt a need to move, to do something immediate, something to break this suffocating stillness.

[story 3]

I stared at the editor's note, my jaw clenched so tightly my molars ached. The sheer audacity of it was breathtaking. When I finally processed the deception, a deep, resonant thrumming started deep within my body. It was a powerful internal shift, a tightening in my limbs that made me want to lean forward, to bridge the gap between the screen and the source of this betrayal. My gaze dropped to my hands, flexing my fingers slowly. A distinct heat began to radiate from my chest, a building pressure that demanded release. I could feel my pulse quickening, a wild, insistent rhythm echoing the turmoil inside me.
